title: Use Try-With-Resources for Resource Management in Java
description: In Java, many resources need to be closed after their usage. Failure to do so results in these resources being considered in use by the operating system, causing memory leaks and performance issues. Java 7 introduced the 'try-with-resources' statement, ensuring the resource in question is closed after usage. This rule checks if closeable resources are not opened using a 'try-with-resources' statement.
recommendation: Always use the 'try-with-resources' statement when dealing with resources that need to be closed after usage. This syntax is safer than traditional methods using try, catch, and finally, and should be preferred.
impact: Not using 'try-with-resources' statements can lead to resource leaks, causing performance issues and potential system instability.

checks.message: Use try-with-resources instead of manual resource closing.
